rationale: The cited paper appears in the introduction as part of a grouped list of prior improvements in preference measurement design and data collection (alongside several other references). The citing paper does not give it a distinct conceptual or methodological role in developing the PIE framework, hypotheses, estimation, or validation tests. Most of the substantive positioning and benchmarking in the article is against traditional conjoint and Luo, Kannan, and Ratchford (2008), not this specific 2008 upgrading paper.
